name: Carrot, raw (food medium)

components.name: total dietary fiber
components.evidence: Fiber 2.8 g/100 g; largely pectin/cellulose, not readily fermentable in a defined medium.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 170393; corroborated Aprifel (~4 g) and Springer 10.1007/s00217-021-03857-0 (sucrose = dominant sugar)
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source in carrot. mM assumes 100 g ~ 100 mL, MW 342.3.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Free glucose. mM assumes 100 g ~ 100 mL, MW 180.16.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Free fructose. mM assumes 100 g ~ 100 mL, MW 180.16.

components.name: total sugars
components.evidence: Sum reference; sucrose > glucose ~ fructose. Maltose/lactose/galactose not detected (0).

components.name: malic acid
components.source: PMC10608851 (Chemical Composition review); Sciendo/Springer carrot UPLC studies; PMC-noted Tiggiano carrot HPLC
components.evidence: PREDOMINANT organic acid of carrot root (L-malate). Total organic-acid pool measured at 1.07-2.79 g/100 g FW (orange varieties toward the low end); malate is the majority of that pool. Individual absolute value not resolvable to a single figure in accessible sources, hence amount=null; this is the single most important omission from the prior FooDB profile.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: Second organic acid; content typically 5-10x lower than malate.

components.name: isocitric acid
components.evidence: Detected in all carrot varieties; minor relative to malate.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence: Detected in all carrot varieties; trace/minor.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Detected in all carrot varieties; trace.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Present in very small amounts (with alpha-ketoglutarate, lactate, glycolate).

components.name: total organic acids
components.evidence: Measured range 1.07-2.79 g/100 g FW; midpoint reported. Malate-dominant. Included as a sourced aggregate because individual acids are not cleanly quantified in accessible sources; treat with caution (carrot is a low-acid vegetable).

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: AA profile. NOTE: was absent from prior FooDB component list; added.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Aspartic acid (free asparagine/aspartate pool is notably high in carrot).

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Glutamic acid; most abundant AA, key N source.

components.name: vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: Retinol-activity equivalents from provitamin-A carotenoids (no preformed retinol in plant).

components.name: lutein + zeaxanthin
components.evidence: Xanthophyll carotenoids; added (absent from prior list).

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Fe (single entry; prior list triple-counted Fe as 'Iron', 'Iron (Fe3+)', 'Fe2+ mitochondria').

components.name: molybdenum (molybdate)
components.evidence: Genuine trace micronutrient; kept though unquantified in USDA panel.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: USDA 18:1 pool; note carrot's characteristic 18:1 is petroselinic acid (18:1 n-12), which USDA lumps into total 18:1.

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: toxic heavy metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Lead
remove.reason: toxic heavy metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Mercury
remove.reason: toxic heavy metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant; carrot not defined by Ni

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace; plants lack B12 so no biological cobalt requirement here

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: ultratrace, non-nutritive/unreliable FooDB value

remove.name: Fluoride
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Iodine c
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace; carrot iodine is negligible

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant metal

remove.name: Barium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant metal

remove.name: Strontium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant metal

remove.name: Creatine cytosol
remove.reason: animal metabolite, biologically implausible in a plant root; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
remove.reason: animal metabolite, implausible in plant; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Naphthalene
remove.reason: PAH environmental contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Formaldehyde
remove.reason: reactive volatile/contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: synthetic pharmaceutical estrogen, impossible native constituent; FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: Diosgenin
remove.reason: steroidal sapogenin of Dioscorea/Trigonella, biologically implausible in carrot; FooDB predicted

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: microbial-fermentation food preservative additive, not a native carrot constituent

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: D-enantiomer implausible as a plant free amino acid; likely FooDB prediction artifact (carrot has L-serine)

remove.name: L-Methionine Sulfoxide
remove.reason: oxidation artifact of methionine, not a distinct nutrient; folded into methionine

remove.name: Levoglutamide
remove.reason: pyroglutamate/processing artifact naming; not a distinct growth nutrient

missing_added: malic acid (predominant organic acid - entirely absent from prior profile)
total organic acids aggregate (1.07-2.79 g/100g FW)
quantitative amounts for sucrose/glucose/fructose/starch (were name-only before)
L-valine (amino acid missing from prior list)
keep_uncovered: petroselinic acid (characteristic carrot 18:1 n-12 fatty acid)
putrescine (genuine trace metabolites, no BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170393 (SR Legacy, Carrots, raw) - full macro/sugar/amino-acid/fatty-acid/vitamin/mineral panel
FooDB FOOD00245 (carrot record, qualitative compound coverage)
PMC10608851 - Chemical Composition, Functional and Anticancer Properties of Carrot (total sugars, total organic acids 1.07-2.79 g/100g FW, malate-dominant)
Springer Eur Food Res Technol doi:10.1007/s00217-021-03857-0 - carrot varieties UPLC (sucrose dominant sugar; malic/citric/isocitric/oxalic/fumaric organic acids)
Aprifel carrot nutritional sheet (sucrose ~4 g, glucose ~1.1 g, fructose ~0.9 g /100g)
PubMed 32083829 - carrot juice profile (L-malate predominant, citric 5-10x lower)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is SUCROSE (3.6 g/100g) with free glucose+fructose (~1.1 g) and starch (1.4 g); the key growth-relevant fix versus the prior FooDB profile is the organic-acid pool (malate-dominant, citric/isocitric/oxalic/fumaric minor) which was entirely missing - reported here with the sourced aggregate (1.07-2.79 g/100g FW) but individual malate/citrate absolutes could not be resolved to single defensible figures, so those amounts are null. Most abundant amino acids are glutamate and aspartate. Confidence downgraded to medium solely because of the unresolved individual organic-acid quantities; the USDA panel (sugars, AAs, vitamins, minerals, fatty acids) is high-confidence.
